response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced senior/staff engineer with nearly two decades of professional work, which is a fundamental mismatch for an entry-level junior position — both in terms of compensation expectations and role fit. Additionally, the role requires strong verbal and written German, but the candidate lists only basic German proficiency. While Java appears in the skills list, the career has centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web technologies rather than professional Java development. The technical caliber is excellent, but this is the wrong role for this profile.
strengths: Exceptional breadth of software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-scale backend systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, agile) directly aligned with quality-focused delivery
Proven collaboration and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, talks, and open-source projects
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level junior role — high risk of misaligned expectations and early attrition
Only basic German, while the role requires strong verbal and written German communication
Java is a peripheral skill; professional depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not Java ecosystems

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and agile experience
